title: ceanothus velutinus subsp. laevigatus
intro: Ceanothus velutinus subsp. laevigatus, also known as Rocky Mountain whitebark or smooth whitebark, is a shrub species in the buckthorn family (Rhamnaceae) native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Ceanothus velutinus subsp. laevigatus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
